anchoring_evidence: The verse anchors directly on the first thread: architectural invariants. The engineering layer's central claim — that the durable customer requirement predates the team and outlasts the language — is a clean engagement with first-principles knowledge applied to engineering. Tags ['architectural-invariant', 'first-principles', 'long-term-thinking', 'spec-vs-behavior'] all fall under the sāṅkhya thread.
hard_edge_preservation: PASS — the chapter's hard edge that 'atman survives change' must not be flattened into 'embrace change' is preserved. The engineering layer is precise: it is not embracing change; it is recognising the durable substrate that the change does not affect. Falsifiability rules out the ossification reading ('using eternal requirement to refuse genuine product evolution').
counter_anti_thesis_check: PASS — the verse does not detach engineers from users (the durable user need is the central anchor), does not float free of the system (the operator is named as a transient custodian, not a free agent), does not flatten svadharma into arbitrary work (the durable customer requirement is the dharma analog).
progressive_build: PASS — at position 12, immediately after the sāṅkhya block opens at 2.11, the verse correctly states the formal eternality claim that 2.11's diagnostic implied. The progression is: 2.11 names the diagnostic of misplaced grief; 2.12 names what the substrate is (the eternal); 2.13 will demonstrate via state-transition analogy. Pedagogical position correct.
